Tests the conversion of a boolean to an object using the Object constructor.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that tests the behavior of the JavaScript `Object` function when applied to a boolean value. It declares a variable `a` and assigns it the boolean value `false`. The script then uses an `assert` statement to verify that converting `a` to an object using `Object(a)` results in a value of type `'object'`. This demonstrates the type conversion behavior of the `Object` function in JavaScript when handling primitive boolean values.
# Global Variables

---
### a
- **Type**: ``boolean``
- **Description**: The variable `a` is a global variable of type `boolean` initialized with the value `false`. It is used in an assertion to check the type of an object created from it.
- **Use**: Used to verify that converting `a` to an object results in an object type.
